rows.notes: Page title at top, very legible year '1825' at right. Clear centred header; no amounts present.
Section heading directly under title; text is clear and there are no monetary figures on this line.
Description mostly legible though surname could be 'Brown' or 'Browne' — read as 'Brown'. Amount columns well aligned; reads '2 15 0' (2 pounds, 15 shillings, 0 pence). Values within valid ranges.

Description is an abbreviation 'Do.' (ditto) then 'Canal. L Day. 1825' — handwriting slightly cramped. Amount appears as '10 2 1/4' with a small farthing mark after the pence; interpreted as 1 and a farthing (1.25 pence). Column alignment reasonable but the farthing symbol is faint, so confidence is moderate.
Clear reading of 'Do. Vicarage Suit Rent'. Amount appears as '4 0 0' and aligns well with the pounds/shillings/pence ruling; digits are distinct.
Entry text 'Hart Hall' clear. Amount reads '1 13 4' (1 pound, 13 shillings, 4 pence). Numbers well aligned and within valid ranges.
Description legible. Amount aligns as '6 6 0' (6 pounds, 6 shillings, 0 pence); digits clear.
Reads 'St. Martin's Do' (ditto). Amount '1 18 0' is clearly written and aligned.
Section heading 'Redeemed Land Tax' (slightly lighter ink) with no monetary figures on this line; serves as a group label.
Entry reads 'Kidlington Rectory'. Amount clearly '22 5 6' (22 pounds, 5 shillings, 6 pence). Alignment of the three monetary columns is clear.
Abbreviated 'Do. Vicarage' is clear. Amount reads '4 19 0' and aligns with the shillings column (19 shillings within valid range).
Section heading 'Mixed Rents, Variable.' centered above the following entries; no amounts on this line.
Large-figure entry 'Kidlington Augmentation' — pounds read as '172' though the hundreds digit is slightly faded. Shillings '14' clear. No pence shown. Alignment consistent with other rows.

Description uncertain: reads 'Garsington - Clinkard' (handwriting could be 'Clinkard'/'Clinkard'/'Chinkard'); small ambiguity. Amount appears as '10 1 1 1/2' — interpreted as 10 pounds, 1 shilling, 1 and a half pence (halfpenny mark after pence). The half-penny symbol is visible but somewhat faint, so confidence is moderate.

Bottom summed total line is clearly underlined and reads '227 10 1 3/4'. The '3/4' is indicated by a three-farthings mark; fraction legible though slightly stylised. Columns align with the other totals.
